Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''{{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound''' located in '''{{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''designated as a National Historic Site''' (as part of '''Takaida Yokoana Tombs'''). Excavated artifacts from this kofun are designated as '''Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Yokoana Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue ware ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Tumulus No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Yokoana Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Tumulus No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Burial Mound === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest forms of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === Excavated Artifacts === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable artifacts include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue ware''' and '''Haji ware''' pieces Some artifacts, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated artifacts were collectively designated as a Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. == Cultural Property == === Kashiwara City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' * '''Excavated artifacts from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', designated on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:高井田山古墳]] [[Horned helmet]] (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}}

{{Kofun in Japan | Name={{ill|Takaidayama Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}} | image=[[File:Takaidayama_Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Stone Chamber | Location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Andō-chō 1-43
(within {{ill|Takaida Yokoana Park|ja|高井田横穴公園}}, a historic site) | Coordinates={{Coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E|region:JP-27_scale:10000}} | Shape={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size=Diameter: 22m | Construction Period=Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burial Facility={{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Buried Individual= | Excavated Artifacts={{ill|Bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, {{ill|Uttō|ja|熨斗}}, {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑}}, {{ill|Tankō|ja|短甲|1=Tankō}}, Glass beads | Historic Site=Part of the '''National Historic Site''' "{{ill|Takaida Yokoana Tombs|ja|高井田横穴}}" | Tangible Cultural Property=Artifacts designated as '''Kashiwara City Cultural Property''' | Notes= | Map2={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Takaidayama
Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''{{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound''' located in '''{{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''designated as a National Historic Site''' (as part of '''Takaida Yokoana Tombs'''). Excavated artifacts from this kofun are designated as '''Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Yokoana Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue ware ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Tumulus No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Yokoana Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Tumulus No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Burial Mound === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest forms of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === Excavated Artifacts === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable artifacts include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue ware''' and '''Haji ware''' pieces Some artifacts, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated artifacts were collectively designated as a Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. == Cultural Property == === Kashiwara City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' * '''Excavated artifacts from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', designated on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:高井田山古墳]] [[Horned helmet]] (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}}

{{Kofun in Japan | Name={{ill|Takaidayama Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}} | image=[[File:Takaidayama_Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Stone Chamber | Location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Andō-chō 1-43
(within {{ill|Takaida Yokoana Park|ja|高井田横穴公園}}, a historic site) | Coordinates={{Coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E|region:JP-27_scale:10000}} | Shape={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size=Diameter: 22m | Construction Period=Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burial Facility={{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Buried Individual= | Excavated Artifacts={{ill|Bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, {{ill|Uttō|ja|熨斗}}, {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑}}, {{ill|Tankō|ja|短甲|1=Tankō}}, Glass beads | Historic Site=Part of the '''National Historic Site''' "{{ill|Takaida Yokoana Tombs|ja|高井田横穴}}" | Tangible Cultural Property=Artifacts designated as '''Kashiwara City Cultural Property''' | Notes= | Map2={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Takaidayama
Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''{{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} burial mound''' located in '''{{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''designated as a National Historic Site''' (as part of '''Takaida Yokoana Tombs'''). Excavated artifacts from this kofun are designated as '''Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Yokoana Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue ware ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Tumulus No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Yokoana Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Tumulus No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Burial Mound === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest forms of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === Excavated Artifacts === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable artifacts include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue ware''' and '''Haji ware''' pieces Some artifacts, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated artifacts were collectively designated as a Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. == Cultural Property == === Kashiwara City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' * '''Excavated artifacts from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', designated on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:高井田山古墳]] [[Horned helmet]] (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}}

revision.text:
{{Kofun in Japan | Name={{ill|Takaidayama Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}} | image=[[File:Takaidayama_Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Stone Chamber | Location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Andō-chō 1-43
(within {{ill|Takaida Yokoana Park|ja|高井田横穴公園}}, a historic site) | Coordinates={{Coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E|region:JP-27_scale:10000}} | Shape={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size=Diameter: 22m | Construction Period=Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burial Facility={{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Buried Individual= | Excavated Artifacts={{ill|Bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, {{ill|Uttō|ja|熨斗}}, {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑}}, {{ill|Tankō|ja|短甲|1=Tankō}}, Glass beads | Historic Site=Part of the '''National Historic Site''' "{{ill|Takaida Yokoana Tombs|ja|高井田横穴}}" | Tangible Cultural Property=Artifacts designated as '''Kashiwara City Cultural Property''' | Notes= | Map2={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Takaidayama
Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''{{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} burial mound''' located in '''{{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''designated as a National Historic Site''' (as part of '''Takaida Yokoana Tombs'''). Excavated artifacts from this kofun are designated as '''Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Yokoana Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue ware ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Tumulus No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Yokoana Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Tumulus No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Burial Mound === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest forms of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === Excavated Artifacts === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable artifacts include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue ware''' and '''Haji ware''' pieces Some artifacts, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated artifacts were collectively designated as a Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. == Cultural Property == === Kashiwara City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' * '''Excavated artifacts from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', designated on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:高井田山古墳]] [[Horned helmet]] (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}}

Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === Excavated Artifacts === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable artifacts include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue ware''' and '''Haji ware''' pieces Some artifacts, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated artifacts were collectively designated as a Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. == Cultural Property == === Kashiwara City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' * '''Excavated artifacts from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', designated on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:高井田山古墳]] [[Horned helmet]] (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}}

revision.text:
{{Kofun in Japan | Name={{ill|Takaidayama Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}} | image=[[File:Takaidayama_Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Stone Chamber | Location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Andō-chō 1-43
(within {{ill|Takaida Yokoana Park|ja|高井田横穴公園}}, a historic site) | Coordinates={{Coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E|region:JP-27_scale:10000}} | Shape={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size=Diameter: 22m | Construction Period=Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burial Facility={{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Buried Individual= | Excavated Artifacts={{ill|Bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, {{ill|Uttō|ja|熨斗}}, {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑}}, {{ill|Tankō|ja|短甲|1=Tankō}}, Glass beads | Historic Site=Part of the '''National Historic Site''' "{{ill|Takaida Yokoana Tombs|ja|高井田横穴}}" | Tangible Cultural Property=Artifacts designated as '''Kashiwara City Cultural Property''' | Notes= | Map2={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Takaidayama
Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''{{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} burial mound''' located in '''{{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''designated as a National Historic Site''' (as part of '''Takaida Yokoana Tombs'''). Excavated artifacts from this kofun are designated as '''Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Yokoana Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue ware ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Tumulus No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Yokoana Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Tumulus No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Burial Mound === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest forms of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === Excavated Artifacts === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable artifacts include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue ware''' and '''Haji ware''' pieces Some artifacts, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated artifacts were collectively designated as a Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. == Cultural Property == === Kashiwara City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' * '''Excavated artifacts from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', designated on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:高井田山古墳]] [[Horned helmet]] (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}}

(within {{ill|Takaida Yokoana Park|ja|高井田横穴公園}}, a historic site) | Coordinates={{Coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E|region:JP-27_scale:10000}} | Shape={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size=Diameter: 22m | Construction Period=Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burial Facility={{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Buried Individual= | Excavated Artifacts={{ill|Bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, {{ill|Uttō|ja|熨斗}}, {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑}}, {{ill|Tankō|ja|短甲|1=Tankō}}, Glass beads | Historic Site=Part of the '''National Historic Site''' "{{ill|Takaida Yokoana Tombs|ja|高井田横穴}}" | Tangible Cultural Property=Artifacts designated as '''Kashiwara City Cultural Property''' | Notes= | Map2={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Takaidayama
Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''{{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} burial mound''' located in '''{{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''designated as a National Historic Site''' (as part of '''Takaida Yokoana Tombs'''). Excavated artifacts from this kofun are designated as '''Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Yokoana Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue ware ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Tumulus No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Yokoana Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Tumulus No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Burial Mound === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest forms of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === Excavated Artifacts === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable artifacts include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue ware''' and '''Haji ware''' pieces Some artifacts, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated artifacts were collectively designated as a Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. == Cultural Property == === Kashiwara City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' * '''Excavated artifacts from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', designated on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:高井田山古墳]] [[Horned helmet]] (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}}

revision.text:
{{Kofun in Japan | Name={{ill|Takaidayama Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}} | image=[[File:Takaidayama_Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Stone Chamber | Location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Andō-chō 1-43
(within {{ill|Takaida Yokoana Park|ja|高井田横穴公園}}, a historic site) | Coordinates={{Coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E|region:JP-27_scale:10000}} | Shape={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size=Diameter: 22m | Construction Period=Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burial Facility={{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Buried Individual= | Excavated Artifacts={{ill|Bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, {{ill|Uttō|ja|熨斗}}, {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑}}, {{ill|Tankō|ja|短甲|1=Tankō}}, Glass beads | Historic Site=Part of the '''National Historic Site''' "{{ill|Takaida Yokoana Tombs|ja|高井田横穴}}" | Tangible Cultural Property=Artifacts designated as '''Kashiwara City Cultural Property''' | Notes= | Map2={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Takaidayama
Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''{{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} burial mound''' located in '''{{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''designated as a National Historic Site''' (as part of '''Takaida Yokoana Tombs'''). Excavated artifacts from this kofun are designated as '''Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Yokoana Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue ware ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Tumulus No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Yokoana Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Tumulus No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Burial Mound === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest forms of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === Excavated Artifacts === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable artifacts include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue ware''' and '''Haji ware''' pieces Some artifacts, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated artifacts were collectively designated as a Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. == Cultural Property == === Kashiwara City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' * '''Excavated artifacts from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', designated on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:高井田山古墳]] [[Horned helmet]] (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}}

{{Kofun in Japan | Name={{ill|Takaidayama Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}} | image=[[File:Takaidayama_Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Stone Chamber | Location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Andō-chō 1-43
(within {{ill|Takaida Yokoana Park|ja|高井田横穴公園}}, a historic site) | Coordinates={{Coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E|region:JP-27_scale:10000}} | Shape={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size=Diameter: 22m | Construction Period=Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burial Facility={{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Buried Individual= | Excavated Artifacts={{ill|Bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, {{ill|Uttō|ja|熨斗}}, {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑}}, {{ill|Tankō|ja|短甲|1=Tankō}}, Glass beads | Historic Site=Part of the '''National Historic Site''' "{{ill|Takaida Yokoana Tombs|ja|高井田横穴}}" | Tangible Cultural Property=Artifacts designated as '''Kashiwara City Cultural Property''' | Notes= | Map2={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Takaidayama
Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''{{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} burial mound''' located in '''{{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''designated as a National Historic Site''' (as part of '''Takaida Yokoana Tombs'''). Excavated artifacts from this kofun are designated as '''Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Yokoana Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue ware ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Tumulus No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Yokoana Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Tumulus No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Burial Mound === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest forms of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === Excavated Artifacts === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable artifacts include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue ware''' and '''Haji ware''' pieces Some artifacts, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated artifacts were collectively designated as a Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. == Cultural Property == === Kashiwara City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' * '''Excavated artifacts from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', designated on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:高井田山古墳]] [[Horned helmet]] (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}}

revision.text:
{{Kofun in Japan | Name={{ill|Takaidayama Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}} | image=[[File:Takaidayama_Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Stone Chamber | Location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Andō-chō 1-43
(within {{ill|Takaida Yokoana Park|ja|高井田横穴公園}}, a historic site) | Coordinates={{Coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E|region:JP-27_scale:10000}} | Shape={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size=Diameter: 22m | Construction Period=Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burial Facility={{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Buried Individual= | Excavated Artifacts={{ill|Bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, {{ill|Uttō|ja|熨斗}}, {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑}}, {{ill|Tankō|ja|短甲|1=Tankō}}, Glass beads | Historic Site=Part of the '''National Historic Site''' "{{ill|Takaida Yokoana Tombs|ja|高井田横穴}}" | Tangible Cultural Property=Artifacts designated as '''Kashiwara City Cultural Property''' | Notes= | Map2={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Takaidayama
Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''{{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} burial mound''' located in '''{{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''designated as a National Historic Site''' (as part of '''Takaida Yokoana Tombs'''). Excavated artifacts from this kofun are designated as '''Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Yokoana Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue ware ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Tumulus No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Yokoana Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Tumulus No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Burial Mound === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest forms of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === Excavated Artifacts === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable artifacts include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue ware''' and '''Haji ware''' pieces Some artifacts, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated artifacts were collectively designated as a Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. == Cultural Property == === Kashiwara City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' * '''Excavated artifacts from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', designated on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:高井田山古墳]] [[Horned helmet]] (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}}

{{Kofun in Japan | Name={{ill|Takaidayama Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}} | image=[[File:Takaidayama_Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Stone Chamber | Location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Andō-chō 1-43
(within {{ill|Takaida Yokoana Park|ja|高井田横穴公園}}, a historic site) | Coordinates={{Coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E|region:JP-27_scale:10000}} | Shape={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size=Diameter: 22m | Construction Period=Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burial Facility={{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Buried Individual= | Excavated Artifacts={{ill|Bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, {{ill|Uttō|ja|熨斗}}, {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑}}, {{ill|Tankō|ja|短甲|1=Tankō}}, Glass beads | Historic Site=Part of the '''National Historic Site''' "{{ill|Takaida Yokoana Tombs|ja|高井田横穴}}" | Tangible Cultural Property=Artifacts designated as '''Kashiwara City Cultural Property''' | Notes= | Map2={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Takaidayama
Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''{{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} burial mound''' located in '''{{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''designated as a National Historic Site''' (as part of '''Takaida Yokoana Tombs'''). Excavated artifacts from this kofun are designated as '''Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Yokoana Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue ware ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Tumulus No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Yokoana Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Tumulus No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Burial Mound === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest forms of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === Excavated Artifacts === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable artifacts include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue ware''' and '''Haji ware''' pieces Some artifacts, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated artifacts were collectively designated as a Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. == Cultural Property == === Kashiwara City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' * '''Excavated artifacts from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', designated on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:高井田山古墳]] [[Horned helmet]] (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}}

{{Kofun in Japan | Name={{ill|Takaidayama Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}} | image=[[File:Takaidayama_Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Stone Chamber | Location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Andō-chō 1-43
(within {{ill|Takaida Yokoana Park|ja|高井田横穴公園}}, a historic site) | Coordinates={{Coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E|region:JP-27_scale:10000}} | Shape={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size=Diameter: 22m | Construction Period=Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burial Facility={{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Buried Individual= | Excavated Artifacts={{ill|Bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, {{ill|Uttō|ja|熨斗}}, {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑}}, {{ill|Tankō|ja|短甲|1=Tankō}}, Glass beads | Historic Site=Part of the '''National Historic Site''' "{{ill|Takaida Yokoana Tombs|ja|高井田横穴}}" | Tangible Cultural Property=Artifacts designated as '''Kashiwara City Cultural Property''' | Notes= | Map2={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Takaidayama
Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''{{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} burial mound''' located in '''{{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''designated as a National Historic Site''' (as part of '''Takaida Yokoana Tombs'''). Excavated artifacts from this kofun are designated as '''Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Yokoana Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue ware ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Tumulus No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Yokoana Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Tumulus No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Burial Mound === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest forms of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === Excavated Artifacts === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable artifacts include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue ware''' and '''Haji ware''' pieces Some artifacts, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated artifacts were collectively designated as a Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. == Cultural Property == === Kashiwara City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' * '''Excavated artifacts from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', designated on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:高井田山古墳]] [[Horned helmet]] (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}}
