Stone Chamber | location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Andō-chō 1-43
(within {{ill|Takaida Yokoana Park|ja|高井田横穴公園}}, a historic site) | coordinates={{Coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E|region:JP-27_map_size:10000}} | type={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area=Diameter: 22m | built=Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | burial={{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Buried Individual= | burial goods={{ill|Bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, {{ill|Uttō|ja|熨斗}}, {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}}, {{ill|Tankō|ja|短甲|1=Tankō}}, Glass beads | other_other_designation=Part of the '''National other_other_designation''' "{{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴}}" | Tangible Cultural Property=burial goods other_designation1 as '''Kashiwara City Cultural Property''' | notes= | map={{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Takaidayama
Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''{{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun''' located in '''{{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''other_designation1 as a National other_other_designation''' (as part of '''Takaida Cave Tombs'''). excavations from this kofun are other_designation1 as '''Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Cave Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue pottery ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Kofun No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National other_other_designation "Takaida Cave Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Kofun No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Kofun === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively other_designation1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. == Cultural Property == === Kashiwara City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', other_designation1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National other_other_designations in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:高井田山古墳]] [[Horned helmet]] (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}}

The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively other_designation1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. == Cultural Property == === Kashiwara City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', other_designation1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National other_other_designations in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:高井田山古墳]] [[Horned helmet]] (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}}

Stone Chamber | location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Andō-chō 1-43
(within {{ill|Takaida Yokoana Park|ja|高井田横穴公園}}, a historic site) | coordinates={{Coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E|region:JP-27_map_size:10000}} | type={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area=Diameter: 22m | built=Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | burial={{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Buried Individual= | burial goods={{ill|Bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, {{ill|Uttō|ja|熨斗}}, {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}}, {{ill|Tankō|ja|短甲|1=Tankō}}, Glass beads | other_other_designation=Part of the '''National other_other_designation''' "{{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴}}" | Tangible Cultural Property=burial goods other_designation1 as '''Kashiwara City Cultural Property''' | notes= | map={{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Takaidayama
Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''{{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun''' located in '''{{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''other_designation1 as a National other_other_designation''' (as part of '''Takaida Cave Tombs'''). excavations from this kofun are other_designation1 as '''Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Cave Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue pottery ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Kofun No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National other_other_designation "Takaida Cave Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Kofun No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Kofun === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively other_designation1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. == Cultural Property == === Kashiwara City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', other_designation1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National other_other_designations in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:高井田山古墳]] [[Horned helmet]] (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}}

Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively other_designation1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. == Cultural Property == === Kashiwara City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', other_designation1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National other_designations in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:高井田山古墳]] [[Horned helmet]] (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Kofun in Japan | name={{ill|Takaidayama Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}} | image=[[File:Takaidayama_Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Stone Chamber | location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Andō-chō 1-43
(within {{ill|Takaida Yokoana Park|ja|高井田横穴公園}}, a historic site) | coordinates={{Coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E|region:JP-27_map_size:10000}} | type={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area=Diameter: 22m | built=Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | burial={{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Buried Individual= | burial goods={{ill|Bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, {{ill|Uttō|ja|熨斗}}, {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}}, {{ill|Tankō|ja|短甲|1=Tankō}}, Glass beads | other_designation=Part of the '''National other_designation''' "{{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴}}" | Tangible Cultural Property=burial goods other_designation1 as '''Kashiwara City Cultural Property''' | notes= | map={{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Takaidayama
Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''{{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun''' located in '''{{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''other_designation1 as a National other_designation''' (as part of '''Takaida Cave Tombs'''). excavations from this kofun are other_designation1 as '''Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Cave Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue pottery ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Kofun No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National other_designation "Takaida Cave Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Kofun No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Kofun === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively other_designation1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. == Cultural Property == === Kashiwara City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', other_designation1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National other_designations in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:高井田山古墳]] [[Horned helmet]] (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}}

While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively other_designation1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. == Cultural Property == === Kashiwara City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', other_designation1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National other_designations in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:高井田山古墳]] [[Horned helmet]] (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}}

(within {{ill|Takaida Yokoana Park|ja|高井田横穴公園}}, a historic site) | coordinates={{Coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E|region:JP-27_map_size:10000}} | type={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area=Diameter: 22m | built=Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | burial={{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Buried Individual= | burial goods={{ill|Bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, {{ill|Uttō|ja|熨斗}}, {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}}, {{ill|Tankō|ja|短甲|1=Tankō}}, Glass beads | other_designation=Part of the '''National other_designation''' "{{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴}}" | Tangible Cultural Property=burial goods other_designation1 as '''Kashiwara City Cultural Property''' | notes= | map={{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Takaidayama
Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''{{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun''' located in '''{{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''other_designation1 as a National other_designation''' (as part of '''Takaida Cave Tombs'''). excavations from this kofun are other_designation1 as '''Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Cave Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue pottery ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Kofun No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National other_designation "Takaida Cave Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Kofun No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Kofun === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively other_designation1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. == Cultural Property == === Kashiwara City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', other_designation1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National other_designations in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:高井田山古墳]] [[Horned helmet]] (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}}

To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Kofun === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively other_designation1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. == Cultural Property == === Kashiwara City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', other_designation1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National other_designations in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:高井田山古墳]] [[Horned helmet]] (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}}

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Kofun in Japan | name={{ill|Takaidayama Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}} | image=[[File:Takaidayama_Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Stone Chamber | location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Andō-chō 1-43
(within {{ill|Takaida Yokoana Park|ja|高井田横穴公園}}, a historic site) | coordinates={{Coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E|region:JP-27_map_size:10000}} | type={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area=Diameter: 22m | built=Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | burial={{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Buried Individual= | burial goods={{ill|Bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, {{ill|Uttō|ja|熨斗}}, {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}}, {{ill|Tankō|ja|短甲|1=Tankō}}, Glass beads | other_designation=Part of the '''National other_designation''' "{{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴}}" | Tangible Cultural Property=burial goods other_designation1 as '''Kashiwara City Cultural Property''' | notes= | map={{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Takaidayama
Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''{{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun''' located in '''{{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''other_designation1 as a National other_designation''' (as part of '''Takaida Cave Tombs'''). excavations from this kofun are other_designation1 as '''Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Cave Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue pottery ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Kofun No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National other_designation "Takaida Cave Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Kofun No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Kofun === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively other_designation1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. == Cultural Property == === Kashiwara City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', other_designation1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National other_designations in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:高井田山古墳]] [[Horned helmet]] (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}}

During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Cave Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue pottery ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Kofun No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National other_designation "Takaida Cave Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Kofun No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Kofun === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively other_designation1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. == Cultural Property == === Kashiwara City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', other_designation1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National other_designations in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:高井田山古墳]] [[Horned helmet]] (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}}

Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''{{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun''' located in '''{{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''other_designation1 as a National other_designation''' (as part of '''Takaida Cave Tombs'''). excavations from this kofun are other_designation1 as '''Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Cave Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue pottery ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Kofun No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National other_designation "Takaida Cave Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Kofun No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Kofun === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively other_designation1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. == Cultural Property == === Kashiwara City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', other_designation1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National other_designations in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:高井田山古墳]] [[Horned helmet]] (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Kofun in Japan | name={{ill|Takaidayama Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}} | image=[[File:Takaidayama_Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Stone Chamber | location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Andō-chō 1-43
(within {{ill|Takaida Yokoana Park|ja|高井田横穴公園}}, a historic site) | coordinates={{Coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E|region:JP-27_map_size:10000}} | type={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area=Diameter: 22m | built=Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | burial={{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Buried Individual= | burial goods={{ill|Bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, {{ill|Uttō|ja|熨斗}}, {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}}, {{ill|Tankō|ja|短甲|1=Tankō}}, Glass beads | other_designation=Part of the '''National other_designation''' "{{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴}}" | Tangible Cultural Property=burial goods other_designation1 as '''Kashiwara City Cultural Property''' | notes= | map={{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Takaidayama
Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''{{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun''' located in '''{{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''other_designation1 as a National other_designation''' (as part of '''Takaida Cave Tombs'''). excavations from this kofun are other_designation1 as '''Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Cave Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue pottery ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Kofun No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National other_designation "Takaida Cave Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Kofun No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Kofun === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively other_designation1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible Cultural Property'''. == Cultural Property == === Kashiwara City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', other_designation1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National other_designations in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:高井田山古墳]] [[Horned helmet]] (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Kofun in Japan | name={{ill|Takaidayama Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}} | image=[[File:Takaidayama_Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Stone Chamber | location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Andō-chō 1-43
(within {{ill|Takaida Yokoana Park|ja|高井田横穴公園}}, a historic site) | coordinates={{Coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E|region:JP-27_map_size:10000}} | type={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area=Diameter: 22m | built=Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | burial={{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Buried Individual= | burial goods={{ill|Bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, {{ill|Uttō|ja|熨斗}}, {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}}, {{ill|Tankō|ja|短甲|1=Tankō}}, Glass beads | other_designation=Part of the '''National other_designation''' "{{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴}}" | Tangible designation1=burial goods other_designation1 as '''Kashiwara City designation1''' | notes= | map={{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Takaidayama
Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''{{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun''' located in '''{{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''other_designation1 as a National other_designation''' (as part of '''Takaida Cave Tombs'''). excavations from this kofun are other_designation1 as '''Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Cave Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue pottery ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Kofun No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National other_designation "Takaida Cave Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Kofun No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Kofun === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively other_designation1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. == designation1 == === Kashiwara City Designated designation1 === * '''Tangible designation1''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', other_designation1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National other_designations in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:高井田山古墳]] {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}} (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}} from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳}}
