Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively Historic Site1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. == designation1 == === Kashiwara City Designated designation1 === * '''Tangible designation1''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', Historic Site1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{small|Kashiwara City, Osaka Prefecture. Exhibited at the Kashiwara ... 13 KB (1,542 words) - 07:20, 19 January 2025 {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Search started pages]] [[Category:Takaidazan Kofun]] [[Category:高井田山古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takaidazan Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takaidazan Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:高井田山古墳]] [[sn:Takaidazan Kofun]] [[sn:akaidazan Kofun]]

revision.comment: 26 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:高井田山古墳 up to rev 100337063
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=高井田山古墳 |画像=[[File:Takaidayama_Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
石室 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[柏原市]]安堂町1番43号
（史跡高井田横穴公園内） |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E|region:JP-27_scale:10000}} |形状=[[円墳]] |規模=直径22m |築造時期=[[5世紀]]後半 |埋葬施設=[[横穴式石室]] |被葬者= |出土品=銅鏡・熨斗・衝角付冑・短甲・ガラス玉 |史跡=国の[[史跡]]「高井田横穴」に包含 |有形文化財=出土品（柏原市指定文化財） |特記事項= |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=高井田山
古墳|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=大阪府内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''高井田山古墳'''（たかいだやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[柏原市]]安堂町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。国の[[史跡]]に指定され（史跡「高井田横穴」のうち）、出土品は柏原市指定[[有形文化財]]に指定されている。[[高井田横穴墓群]]の史跡整備に伴い[[発掘調査]]が実施され、古墳時代中期の古墳であることが判明した。[[近畿地方]]で初期の段階に属する[[横穴式石室]]の遺構が検出され、銅製の熨斗（うっと）、[[銅鏡]]、ガラス玉、[[甲冑]]、武器、[[須恵器]]など様々な遺物が出土している。 == 概要== === 発掘調査の経緯=== 高井田山古墳は、1974年（昭和49年）の分布調査によって古墳時代後期の代表的な[[群集墳]]である[[平尾山古墳群]]の高井田支群第2支群56号墳（円墳）として確認され、遺跡分布地図に記入されていた。この古墳と隣接する国の[[史跡]]「高井田横穴墓群」（平尾山古墳群に近接し、時期的にも並行するので平尾山古墳群に含める考えもある）が所在する地区付近を区画整理し開発する計画があり、同横穴墓群の隣接地が開発された場合、水脈や植生の変化により横穴墓の風化を助長させることも懸念されたため、横穴墓群の史跡保存区域を3000平方メートル広げることとなった。そして、この区域に、平尾山古墳群高井田支群第2支群56号墳も含まれることとなった。この古墳を横穴墓群とともに史跡公園の一部として整備、保存するために1990年（平成2年）に柏原市教育委員会によって発掘調査されることとなり、古墳の全貌が明らかにされることになった。 ===墳丘=== 墳丘の北側は宅地として造成され崖となっており、西側は道路を設けた際に削られ、急斜面になっている。南側と西側は人為的な改変を被っていないが発掘箇所のほとんどでは表土下数十センチで地山となっており、盛土の大半が流失しており、[[前方後円墳]]の可能性もあるが一応、直径22メートルの円墳と推定された。石室内の土砂からは[[円筒埴輪]]や[[朝顔形埴輪]]さらに蓋形埴輪が合わせて9個体分出土しているが、後述する横穴式石室の上部が盗掘などにより崩落した時に墳頂部から転落したものと考えられる。 ===横穴式石室=== [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 墳丘頂部で多数の石材が検出され、石室の存在が確認された。石室は地山を平坦に整形して掘り込まれた東西8メートル、南北9メートル前後の楕円形の墓坑上に構築された横穴式石室である。石室の上部は破壊崩落しており、玄室内に多数の石材が転落していた。石室の構造は扁平な板石を積み上げて築かれた片袖式横穴式石室である。玄室の規模は、長さ3.73メートル、幅は奥壁で2.34メートル、玄門部で2.26メートル、やや奥に開く長方形平面を呈する。羨道部は閉塞石をそのまま保存したため正確なところは不明であるが、長さ2.0メートル、幅は玄門部で1.18メートルを測る。玄室の高さは奥壁の残存部で約1.3メートルを測る。構造的には近畿地方の横穴式石室の最古の時期に属する[[藤の森古墳]]との類似点や[[朝鮮半島]][[百済]]の初期横穴式石室の影響も指摘されている。[[白石太一郎]]のように当古墳の横穴式石室を畿内型横穴式石室の最古型式とする考えもある。また一瀬和雄のように出土須恵器の編年から見て藤の森古墳の石室より1段階降るとしながらも、玄室が藤の森古墳より幅広のプランの高井田山古墳の石室が後の近畿地方の大型横穴式石室の系譜の初現ではないかという指摘もある一瀬(1968)pp.63-68。 ===出土遺物=== 石室内は盗掘により、かなり攪乱されていたが木棺に使用されたと考えられる鉄製の鎹（かすがい）の分布状況、副葬品の配置などから2つの木棺が置かれていたことが推定された。玄室の中央に置かれた棺の位置からは純金製耳環1個、[[直刀|鉄刀]]1口が検出されている。玄室東側壁に沿って置かれたもう1つの棺は棺材にコウヤマキを使用しており、棺に伴う遺物として、神人龍虎画像鏡1面（中国鏡　直径20.6センチ）、熨斗1口、純金製耳環2個、鉄刀1口、刀子2口、ガラス玉が5群に分かれて検出されている。2つの棺の周囲の石室内からは槍と判断される鉄製品が破片も含めて7点、矛が9点、槍または矛の附属金具である石突18点以上、鉄鏃多数、横矧板鋲留[[衝角付冑]]、頸甲、肩甲、小札、馬具である鐙の破片、鉸具、鎌、釶、刀子、金銅片、須恵器多数、土師器などが出土している。熨斗は近年までアイロンとして利用されていた火熨斗（ひのし）の形をした銅製品で中国では[[漢代]]以降、朝鮮半島では5世紀以降の墳墓からしばしば出土するものであるが、日本国内の古墳からは[[新沢千塚古墳群]]の126号墳など4例しか出土していないものである。[[朝鮮半島]]百済の[[武寧王]]の陵墓からは当古墳出土品と最も良く似た熨斗が出土している。また、木棺から出土したガラス玉の内1個は金装ガラス（直径12ミリ）で心材のガラス管と外側のガラス管の間に金箔が挟まれている。日本の古墳では、やはり新沢126号墳を始め5例しか出土例がない。また上記の武寧王陵からは同種のものが大量に出土している。出土品は2008年（平成20年）に一括して柏原市指定有形文化財に指定されている[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表（大阪府公式サイト）]。 高井田山古墳出土 神人龍虎画像鏡.JPG|神人龍虎画像鏡
{{small|柏原市立歴史資料館展示（他画像も同様）。}} 高井田山古墳出土 熨斗-2.JPG|熨斗 高井田山古墳出土 装身具類.JPG|装身具類 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|横矧板鋲留衝角付冑 高井田山古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|須恵器 ===築造年代=== 古墳時代中期以降の古墳の築造年代を推定するため最も手がかりとしやすい[[須恵器]]は壺、器台、脚付壺、有蓋高坏、無蓋高坏、[[ハソウ]]などが出土している。これらの須恵器の型式は[[田辺昭三]]による[[陶邑窯]]出土須恵器の[[編年]]（陶邑編年）のTK23型式からTK47型式に位置付けられるもので、西暦480-490年頃が考えられるという。平尾山古墳群や高井田横穴墓群より半世紀近くも先行する時期の古墳であることが明らかとなった。 ===被葬者像=== 当古墳の被葬者としては横穴式石室の構造、規模が韓国宋山里古墳群など百済の王族クラスの古墳の石室に類似し、出土遺物にも熨斗のように共通点があることから百済からの技術工人らと共に渡来した集団の長で、王族に匹敵する人物を想定する説もある。具体的な被葬者としては、特に[[昆支王]]の墓に比定する説がある[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2019050300044/ 安宿郡の古墳と寺院～10～]（柏原市ホームページ）。。 == 文化財 == === 柏原市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 高井田山古墳出土品（考古資料） - 明細は以下。柏原市立歴史資料館保管。2008年（平成20年）3月21日指定[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082200058/?doc_id=1496 高井田山古墳出土品]（柏原市ホームページ）。。 {{hidden begin |title = 柏原市指定有形文化財「高井田山古墳出土品」の明細 |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; ; background:lightgrey; }} * 神人龍虎画像鏡 1点 * 熨斗 1点 * 耳環 3点 * ガラス玉 322点 * 鉄刀 8点 * 槍 7点 * 矛 9点 * 石突 20点 * 鉄鏃 456点 * 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑 1点 * 頸甲 一式（3点） * 肩甲 一式（4点） * 横矧板鋲留短甲 一式（18点） * 小札 383点 * 馬具 一式（25点） * 鎌 1点 * 鉇 1点 * 刀子 6点 * 布付鉄片 1点 * 不明金属片 1点 * 鉄釘 58点 * 鎹 43点 * 須恵器 23点 * 土師器 2点 * 埴輪 26点 * 木心布巻製品 2点 * 漆膜 一式 * 繊維 一式（21点） * 石鏃 1点 {{hidden end}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==参考文献== *安村俊史・桑野一幸『高井田山古墳』柏原市教育委員会 1996年 *一瀬和雄「高井田山型横穴式石室」『大王墓と前方後円墳』[[吉川弘文館]] 63-68頁 ==関連項目== *[[高井田横穴墓群]] *[[平尾山古墳群]] ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html 近畿の横穴式石室資料集成<大阪府河内>]（横穴式石室研究会） {{デフォルトソート:たかいたやまこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:柏原市の歴史]] [[category:円墳]] [[category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=高井田山古墳 |画像=[[File:Takaidayama_Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
石室 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[柏原市]]安堂町1番43号
（史跡高井田横穴公園内） |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E|region:JP-27_scale:10000}} |形状=[[円墳]] |規模=直径22m |築造時期=[[5世紀]]後半 |埋葬施設=[[横穴式石室]] |被葬者= |出土品=銅鏡・熨斗・衝角付冑・短甲・ガラス玉 |史跡=国の[[史跡]]「高井田横穴」に包含 |有形文化財=出土品（柏原市指定文化財） |特記事項= |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=高井田山
古墳|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=大阪府内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''高井田山古墳'''（たかいだやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[柏原市]]安堂町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。国の[[史跡]]に指定され（史跡「高井田横穴」のうち）、出土品は柏原市指定[[有形文化財]]に指定されている。[[高井田横穴墓群]]の史跡整備に伴い[[発掘調査]]が実施され、古墳時代中期の古墳であることが判明した。[[近畿地方]]で初期の段階に属する[[横穴式石室]]の遺構が検出され、銅製の熨斗（うっと）、[[銅鏡]]、ガラス玉、[[甲冑]]、武器、[[須恵器]]など様々な遺物が出土している。 == 概要== === 発掘調査の経緯=== 高井田山古墳は、1974年（昭和49年）の分布調査によって古墳時代後期の代表的な[[群集墳]]である[[平尾山古墳群]]の高井田支群第2支群56号墳（円墳）として確認され、遺跡分布地図に記入されていた。この古墳と隣接する国の[[史跡]]「高井田横穴墓群」（平尾山古墳群に近接し、時期的にも並行するので平尾山古墳群に含める考えもある）が所在する地区付近を区画整理し開発する計画があり、同横穴墓群の隣接地が開発された場合、水脈や植生の変化により横穴墓の風化を助長させることも懸念されたため、横穴墓群の史跡保存区域を3000平方メートル広げることとなった。そして、この区域に、平尾山古墳群高井田支群第2支群56号墳も含まれることとなった。この古墳を横穴墓群とともに史跡公園の一部として整備、保存するために1990年（平成2年）に柏原市教育委員会によって発掘調査されることとなり、古墳の全貌が明らかにされることになった。 ===墳丘=== 墳丘の北側は宅地として造成され崖となっており、西側は道路を設けた際に削られ、急斜面になっている。南側と西側は人為的な改変を被っていないが発掘箇所のほとんどでは表土下数十センチで地山となっており、盛土の大半が流失しており、[[前方後円墳]]の可能性もあるが一応、直径22メートルの円墳と推定された。石室内の土砂からは[[円筒埴輪]]や[[朝顔形埴輪]]さらに蓋形埴輪が合わせて9個体分出土しているが、後述する横穴式石室の上部が盗掘などにより崩落した時に墳頂部から転落したものと考えられる。 ===横穴式石室=== [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 墳丘頂部で多数の石材が検出され、石室の存在が確認された。石室は地山を平坦に整形して掘り込まれた東西8メートル、南北9メートル前後の楕円形の墓坑上に構築された横穴式石室である。石室の上部は破壊崩落しており、玄室内に多数の石材が転落していた。石室の構造は扁平な板石を積み上げて築かれた片袖式横穴式石室である。玄室の規模は、長さ3.73メートル、幅は奥壁で2.34メートル、玄門部で2.26メートル、やや奥に開く長方形平面を呈する。羨道部は閉塞石をそのまま保存したため正確なところは不明であるが、長さ2.0メートル、幅は玄門部で1.18メートルを測る。玄室の高さは奥壁の残存部で約1.3メートルを測る。構造的には近畿地方の横穴式石室の最古の時期に属する[[藤の森古墳]]との類似点や[[朝鮮半島]][[百済]]の初期横穴式石室の影響も指摘されている。[[白石太一郎]]のように当古墳の横穴式石室を畿内型横穴式石室の最古型式とする考えもある。また一瀬和雄のように出土須恵器の編年から見て藤の森古墳の石室より1段階降るとしながらも、玄室が藤の森古墳より幅広のプランの高井田山古墳の石室が後の近畿地方の大型横穴式石室の系譜の初現ではないかという指摘もある一瀬(1968)pp.63-68。 ===出土遺物=== 石室内は盗掘により、かなり攪乱されていたが木棺に使用されたと考えられる鉄製の鎹（かすがい）の分布状況、副葬品の配置などから2つの木棺が置かれていたことが推定された。玄室の中央に置かれた棺の位置からは純金製耳環1個、[[直刀|鉄刀]]1口が検出されている。玄室東側壁に沿って置かれたもう1つの棺は棺材にコウヤマキを使用しており、棺に伴う遺物として、神人龍虎画像鏡1面（中国鏡　直径20.6センチ）、熨斗1口、純金製耳環2個、鉄刀1口、刀子2口、ガラス玉が5群に分かれて検出されている。2つの棺の周囲の石室内からは槍と判断される鉄製品が破片も含めて7点、矛が9点、槍または矛の附属金具である石突18点以上、鉄鏃多数、横矧板鋲留[[衝角付冑]]、頸甲、肩甲、小札、馬具である鐙の破片、鉸具、鎌、釶、刀子、金銅片、須恵器多数、土師器などが出土している。熨斗は近年までアイロンとして利用されていた火熨斗（ひのし）の形をした銅製品で中国では[[漢代]]以降、朝鮮半島では5世紀以降の墳墓からしばしば出土するものであるが、日本国内の古墳からは[[新沢千塚古墳群]]の126号墳など4例しか出土していないものである。[[朝鮮半島]]百済の[[武寧王]]の陵墓からは当古墳出土品と最も良く似た熨斗が出土している。また、木棺から出土したガラス玉の内1個は金装ガラス（直径12ミリ）で心材のガラス管と外側のガラス管の間に金箔が挟まれている。日本の古墳では、やはり新沢126号墳を始め5例しか出土例がない。また上記の武寧王陵からは同種のものが大量に出土している。出土品は2008年（平成20年）に一括して柏原市指定有形文化財に指定されている[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表（大阪府公式サイト）]。 高井田山古墳出土 神人龍虎画像鏡.JPG|神人龍虎画像鏡
{{small|柏原市立歴史資料館展示（他画像も同様）。}} 高井田山古墳出土 熨斗-2.JPG|熨斗 高井田山古墳出土 装身具類.JPG|装身具類 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|横矧板鋲留衝角付冑 高井田山古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|須恵器 ===築造年代=== 古墳時代中期以降の古墳の築造年代を推定するため最も手がかりとしやすい[[須恵器]]は壺、器台、脚付壺、有蓋高坏、無蓋高坏、[[ハソウ]]などが出土している。これらの須恵器の型式は[[田辺昭三]]による[[陶邑窯]]出土須恵器の[[編年]]（陶邑編年）のTK23型式からTK47型式に位置付けられるもので、西暦480-490年頃が考えられるという。平尾山古墳群や高井田横穴墓群より半世紀近くも先行する時期の古墳であることが明らかとなった。 ===被葬者像=== 当古墳の被葬者としては横穴式石室の構造、規模が韓国宋山里古墳群など百済の王族クラスの古墳の石室に類似し、出土遺物にも熨斗のように共通点があることから百済からの技術工人らと共に渡来した集団の長で、王族に匹敵する人物を想定する説もある。具体的な被葬者としては、特に[[昆支王]]の墓に比定する説がある[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2019050300044/ 安宿郡の古墳と寺院～10～]（柏原市ホームページ）。。 == 文化財 == === 柏原市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 高井田山古墳出土品（考古資料） - 明細は以下。柏原市立歴史資料館保管。2008年（平成20年）3月21日指定[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082200058/?doc_id=1496 高井田山古墳出土品]（柏原市ホームページ）。。 {{hidden begin |title = 柏原市指定有形文化財「高井田山古墳出土品」の明細 |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; ; background:lightgrey; }} * 神人龍虎画像鏡 1点 * 熨斗 1点 * 耳環 3点 * ガラス玉 322点 * 鉄刀 8点 * 槍 7点 * 矛 9点 * 石突 20点 * 鉄鏃 456点 * 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑 1点 * 頸甲 一式（3点） * 肩甲 一式（4点） * 横矧板鋲留短甲 一式（18点） * 小札 383点 * 馬具 一式（25点） * 鎌 1点 * 鉇 1点 * 刀子 6点 * 布付鉄片 1点 * 不明金属片 1点 * 鉄釘 58点 * 鎹 43点 * 須恵器 23点 * 土師器 2点 * 埴輪 26点 * 木心布巻製品 2点 * 漆膜 一式 * 繊維 一式（21点） * 石鏃 1点 {{hidden end}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==参考文献== *安村俊史・桑野一幸『高井田山古墳』柏原市教育委員会 1996年 *一瀬和雄「高井田山型横穴式石室」『大王墓と前方後円墳』[[吉川弘文館]] 63-68頁 ==関連項目== *[[高井田横穴墓群]] *[[平尾山古墳群]] ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html 近畿の横穴式石室資料集成<大阪府河内>]（横穴式石室研究会） {{デフォルトソート:たかいたやまこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:柏原市の歴史]] [[category:円墳]] [[category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]]

(within {{ill|Takaida Yokoana Park|ja|高井田横穴公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a historic site) | coordinates={{Coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E|region:JP-27_map_size:10000}} | type={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area=Diameter: 22m | built=Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | burial={{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Interred_individual= | burial goods={{ill|Bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Uttō|ja|熨斗|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tankō|ja|短甲|1=Tankō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Glass beads | Historic Site=Part of the '''National Historic Site''' "{{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" | Tangible designation1=burial goods Historic Site1 as '''Kashiwara City designation1''' | notes= | map={{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Takaidayama
Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''{{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kofun''' located in '''{{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site''' (as part of '''Takaida Cave Tombs'''). excavations from this kofun are Historic Site1 as '''Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Cave Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue pottery ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Kofun No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Cave Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Kofun No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Kofun === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively Historic Site1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. == designation1 == === Kashiwara City Designated designation1 === * '''Tangible designation1''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', Historic Site1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

(within {{ill|Takaida Yokoana Park|ja|高井田横穴公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a historic site) | coordinates={{Coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E|region:JP-27_map_size:10000}} | type={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area=Diameter: 22m | built=Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | burial={{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Interred_individual= | burial goods={{ill|Bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Uttō|ja|熨斗|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tankō|ja|短甲|1=Tankō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Glass beads | Historic Site=Part of the '''National Historic Site''' "{{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" | Tangible designation1=burial goods Historic Site1 as '''Kashiwara City designation1''' | notes= | map={{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Takaidayama
Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''{{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kofun''' located in '''{{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site''' (as part of '''Takaida Cave Tombs'''). excavations from this kofun are Historic Site1 as '''Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Cave Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue pottery ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Kofun No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Cave Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Kofun No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Kofun === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively Historic Site1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. == designation1 == === Kashiwara City Designated designation1 === * '''Tangible designation1''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', Historic Site1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Cave Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Kofun No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Kofun === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively Historic Site1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. == designation1 == === Kashiwara City Designated designation1 === * '''Tangible designation1''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', Historic Site1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

Stone Chamber | location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Andō-chō 1-43
(within {{ill|Takaida Yokoana Park|ja|高井田横穴公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a historic site) | coordinates={{Coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E|region:JP-27_map_size:10000}} | type={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area=Diameter: 22m | built=Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | burial={{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Interred_individual= | burial goods={{ill|Bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Uttō|ja|熨斗|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tankō|ja|短甲|1=Tankō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Glass beads | Historic Site=Part of the '''National Historic Site''' "{{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" | Tangible designation1=burial goods Historic Site1 as '''Kashiwara City designation1''' | notes= | map={{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Takaidayama
Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''{{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kofun''' located in '''{{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site''' (as part of '''Takaida Cave Tombs'''). excavations from this kofun are Historic Site1 as '''Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Cave Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue pottery ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Kofun No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Cave Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Kofun No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Kofun === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively Historic Site1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. == designation1 == === Kashiwara City Designated designation1 === * '''Tangible designation1''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', Historic Site1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively Historic Site1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. == designation1 == === Kashiwara City Designated designation1 === * '''Tangible designation1''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', Historic Site1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

Stone Chamber | location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara, Osaka|qq=}}, Andō-chō 1-43
(within {{ill|Takaida Yokoana Park|ja|高井田横穴公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Yokoana Park|qq=}}, a historic site) | coordinates={{Coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E|region:JP-27_map_size:10000}} | type={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} | area=Diameter: 22m | built=Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th century|qq=}} | burial={{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Interred_individual= | burial goods={{ill|Bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirror|qq=}}, {{ill|Uttō|ja|熨斗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uttō|qq=}}, {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}}, {{ill|Tankō|ja|短甲|1=Tankō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tankō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tankō|qq=}}, Glass beads | Historic Site=Part of the '''National Historic Site''' "{{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Cave Tombs|qq=}}" | Tangible designation1=burial goods Historic Site1 as '''Kashiwara City designation1''' | notes= | map={{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Takaidayama
Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''{{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} Kofun''' located in '''{{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara, Osaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site''' (as part of '''Takaida Cave Tombs'''). excavations from this kofun are Historic Site1 as '''Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Cave Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue pottery ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Kofun No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Cave Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Kofun No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Kofun === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujinomori Kofun|qq=}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively Historic Site1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. == designation1 == === Kashiwara City Designated designation1 === * '''Tangible designation1''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', Historic Site1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Cave Tombs|qq=}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaidazan Kofun|qq=}}

Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively Historic Site1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. == designation1 == === Kashiwara City Designated designation1 === * '''Tangible designation1''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', Historic Site1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Cave Tombs|qq=}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaidazan Kofun|qq=}}

Stone Chamber | location=[[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], Andō-chō 1-43
(within {{ill|Takaida Yokoana Park|ja|高井田横穴公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Yokoana Park|qq=}}, a historic site) | coordinates={{Coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E|region:JP-27_map_size:10000}} | type={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} | area=Diameter: 22m | built=Late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] | burial={{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Interred_individual= | burial goods=[[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], [[:en:Noshi|Uttō]], {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}}, [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]], Glass beads | Historic Site=Part of the '''National Historic Site''' "{{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Cave Tombs|qq=}}" | Tangible designation1=burial goods Historic Site1 as '''Kashiwara City designation1''' | notes= | map={{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Takaidayama
Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''[[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun''' located in '''[[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site''' (as part of '''Takaida Cave Tombs'''). excavations from this kofun are Historic Site1 as '''Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Cave Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue pottery ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Kofun No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Cave Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Kofun No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Kofun === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujinomori Kofun|qq=}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively Historic Site1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. == designation1 == === Kashiwara City Designated designation1 === * '''Tangible designation1''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', Historic Site1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Takaida Cave Tombs|Takaida Cave Tombs]] * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaidazan Kofun|qq=}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Kofun in Japan | name={{ill|Takaidayama Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaidayama Kofun|qq=}} | image=[[File:Takaidayama_Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Stone Chamber | location=[[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], Andō-chō 1-43
(within {{ill|Takaida Yokoana Park|ja|高井田横穴公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Yokoana Park|qq=}}, a historic site) | coordinates={{Coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E|region:JP-27_map_size:10000}} | type={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} | area=Diameter: 22m | built=Late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] | burial={{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Interred_individual= | burial goods=[[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], [[:en:Noshi|Uttō]], {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}}, [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]], Glass beads | Historic Site=Part of the '''National Historic Site''' "{{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Cave Tombs|qq=}}" | Tangible designation1=burial goods Historic Site1 as '''Kashiwara City designation1''' | notes= | map={{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Takaidayama
Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''[[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun''' located in '''[[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site''' (as part of '''Takaida Cave Tombs'''). excavations from this kofun are Historic Site1 as '''Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Cave Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue pottery ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Kofun No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Cave Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Kofun No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Kofun === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujinomori Kofun|qq=}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively Historic Site1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. == designation1 == === Kashiwara City Designated designation1 === * '''Tangible designation1''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', Historic Site1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Takaida Cave Tombs|Takaida Cave Tombs]] * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaidazan Kofun|qq=}}
{{small|Kashiwara City, Osaka Prefecture. Exhibited at the Kashiwara ... 13 KB (1,542 words) - 07:20, 19 January 2025 {{translated page|ja|高井田山古墳|version=100337063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Search started pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Takaidazan Kofun]][[sn:Takaidazan Kofun]]}} [[sn:Takaidazan Kofun]] [[ja:高井田山古墳]] [[sn:akaidazan Kofun]] [[Category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]]
