(within {{ill|Takaida Yokoana Park|ja|高井田横穴公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Yokoana Park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a historic site) | coordinates={{Coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E|region:JP-27_map_size:10000}} | type={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area=Diameter: 22m | built=Late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] | burial={{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Interred_individual= | burial goods=[[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], [[:en:Noshi|Uttō]], {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}}, [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]], Glass beads | Historic Site=Part of the '''National Historic Site''' "{{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Cave Tombs|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" | Tangible designation1=burial goods Historic Site1 as '''Kashiwara City designation1''' | notes= | map={{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Takaidayama
Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''[[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun''' located in '''[[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site''' (as part of '''Takaida Cave Tombs'''). excavations from this kofun are Historic Site1 as '''Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Cave Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue pottery ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Kofun No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Cave Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Kofun No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Kofun === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujinomori Kofun|qq=}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively Historic Site1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. == designation1 == === Kashiwara City Designated designation1 === * '''Tangible designation1''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', Historic Site1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Takaida Cave Tombs|Takaida Cave Tombs]] * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaidazan Kofun|qq=}}

(within {{ill|Takaida Yokoana Park|ja|高井田横穴公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Yokoana Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a historic site) | coordinates={{Coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E|region:JP-27_map_size:10000}} | type={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area=Diameter: 22m | built=Late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] | burial={{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Interred_individual= | burial goods=[[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], [[:en:Noshi|Uttō]], {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}}, [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]], Glass beads | Historic Site=Part of the '''National Historic Site''' "{{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Cave Tombs|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" | Tangible designation1=burial goods Historic Site1 as '''Kashiwara City designation1''' | notes= | map={{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Takaidayama
Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''[[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun''' located in '''[[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site''' (as part of '''Takaida Cave Tombs'''). excavations from this kofun are Historic Site1 as '''Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Cave Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue pottery ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Kofun No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Cave Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Kofun No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Kofun === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujinomori Kofun|qq=}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively Historic Site1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. == designation1 == === Kashiwara City Designated designation1 === * '''Tangible designation1''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', Historic Site1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Takaida Cave Tombs|Takaida Cave Tombs]] * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaidazan Kofun|qq=}}

Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''[[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun''' located in '''[[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site''' (as part of '''Takaida Cave Tombs'''). excavations from this kofun are Historic Site1 as '''Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Cave Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue pottery ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Kofun No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Cave Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Kofun No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Kofun === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujinomori Kofun|qq=}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively Historic Site1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. == designation1 == === Kashiwara City Designated designation1 === * '''Tangible designation1''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', Historic Site1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Takaida Cave Tombs|Takaida Cave Tombs]] * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaidazan Kofun|qq=}}
{{small|Kashiwara City, Osaka Prefecture. Exhibited at the Kashiwara ... 13 KB (1,542 words) - 07:20, 19 January 2025 {{translated page|ja|高井田山古墳|version=100337063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:高井田山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Takaidayama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11668715}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''[[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun''' located in '''[[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site''' (as part of '''Takaida Cave Tombs'''). excavations from this kofun are Historic Site1 as '''Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Cave Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue pottery ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Kofun No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Cave Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Kofun No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Kofun === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujinomori Kofun|qq=}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively Historic Site1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. == designation1 == === Kashiwara City Designated designation1 === * '''Tangible designation1''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', Historic Site1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Takaida Cave Tombs|Takaida Cave Tombs]] * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaidazan Kofun|qq=}}

Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''[[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun''' located in '''[[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site''' (as part of '''Takaida Cave Tombs'''). excavations from this kofun are Historic Site1 as '''Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Cave Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue pottery ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Kofun No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Cave Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Kofun No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Kofun === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujinomori Kofun|qq=}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively Historic Site1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. == designation1 == === Kashiwara City Designated designation1 === * '''Tangible designation1''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', Historic Site1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Takaida Cave Tombs|Takaida Cave Tombs]] * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaidazan Kofun|qq=}}

Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''[[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun''' located in '''[[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site''' (as part of '''Takaida Cave Tombs'''). excavations from this kofun are Historic Site1 as '''Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Cave Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue pottery ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Kofun No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Cave Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Kofun No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Kofun === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujinomori Kofun|qq=}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively Historic Site1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. == designation1 == === Kashiwara City Designated designation1 === * '''Tangible designation1''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', Historic Site1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Takaida Cave Tombs|Takaida Cave Tombs]] * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaidazan Kofun|qq=}}

Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''[[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun''' located in '''[[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site''' (as part of '''Takaida Cave Tombs'''). excavations from this kofun are Historic Site1 as '''Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Cave Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue pottery ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Kofun No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Cave Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Kofun No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Kofun === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujinomori Kofun|qq=}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively Historic Site1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. == designation1 == === Kashiwara City Designated designation1 === * '''Tangible designation1''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', Historic Site1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Takaida Cave Tombs|Takaida Cave Tombs]] * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaidazan Kofun|qq=}}

Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''[[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun''' located in '''[[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site''' (as part of '''Takaida Cave Tombs'''). excavations from this kofun are Historic Site1 as '''Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Cave Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue pottery ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Kofun No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Cave Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Kofun No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Kofun === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujinomori Kofun|qq=}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively Historic Site1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. == designation1 == === Kashiwara City Designated designation1 === * '''Tangible designation1''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', Historic Site1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Takaida Cave Tombs|Takaida Cave Tombs]] * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaidazan Kofun|qq=}}

Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''[[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun''' located in '''[[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site''' (as part of '''Takaida Cave Tombs'''). excavations from this kofun are Historic Site1 as '''Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Cave Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue pottery ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Kofun No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Cave Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Kofun No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Kofun === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujinomori Kofun|qq=}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively Historic Site1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. == designation1 == === Kashiwara City Designated designation1 === * '''Tangible designation1''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', Historic Site1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Takaida Cave Tombs|Takaida Cave Tombs]] * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaidazan Kofun|qq=}}

Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''[[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun''' located in '''[[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site''' (as part of '''Takaida Cave Tombs'''). excavations from this kofun are Historic Site1 as '''Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Cave Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue pottery ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Kofun No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Cave Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Kofun No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Kofun === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujinomori Kofun|qq=}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively Historic Site1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. == designation1 == === Kashiwara City Designated designation1 === * '''Tangible designation1''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', Historic Site1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Takaida Cave Tombs|Takaida Cave Tombs]] * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaidazan Kofun|qq=}}
{{small|Kashiwara City, Osaka Prefecture. Exhibited at the Kashiwara ... 13 KB (1,542 words) - 07:20, 19 January 2025 {{translated page|ja|高井田山古墳|version=100337063|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:高井田山古墳]] {{Commons category|Takaidayama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11668715}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively Historic Site1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. == designation1 == === Kashiwara City Designated designation1 === * '''Tangible designation1''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', Historic Site1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|Takaida Cave Tombs|WD=Q11668714|lt=Takaida Cave Tombs}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaidazan Kofun|qq=}}

(within {{ill|Takaida Yokoana Park|ja|高井田横穴公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Yokoana Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a historic site) | coordinates={{Coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E|region:JP-27_map_size:10000}} | type={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area=Diameter: 22m | built=Late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}} | burial={{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Interred_individual= | burial goods={{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}, {{ill|Noshi|en|Noshi|WD=Q1511688|lt=Uttō}}, {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}}, {{ill|Tankō|en|Tankō|WD=Q1687003|lt=Tankō}}, Glass beads | Historic Site=Part of the '''National Historic Site''' "{{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|ja|高井田横穴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Cave Tombs|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" | Tangible designation1=burial goods Historic Site1 as '''Kashiwara City designation1''' | notes= | map={{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Takaidayama
Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=23.0|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22|N|135|38|23|E}}}} }} '''Takaidayama Kofun''' (高井田山古墳) is a '''{{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} Kofun''' located in '''{{ill|Kashiwara|en|Kashiwara|WD=Q873583|lt=Kashiwara, Osaka}}, Osaka Prefecture'''. It is a '''round tumulus''' (円墳) and is '''Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site''' (as part of '''Takaida Cave Tombs'''). excavations from this kofun are Historic Site1 as '''Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. During '''archaeological surveys conducted as part of the preservation of the Takaida Cave Tombs''', it was confirmed that this tumulus dates to the '''mid-Kofun period'''. The site revealed '''one of the earliest Yokoana-style stone chambers in the Kinki region''', along with significant grave goods such as '''bronze mirrors, glass beads, armor, weapons, and Sue pottery ceramics'''. == Overview == === Excavation History === Takaidayama Kofun was first identified in a '''1974 (Shōwa 49) survey''' as '''Kofun No. 56''' within '''Group 2 of the Takaida Branch of the Hiraozan Kofun Cluster'''. It was marked on '''archaeological distribution maps''' as a '''late-Kofun period tumulus'''. Plans for land development near the '''National Historic Site "Takaida Cave Tombs"''' raised concerns about the potential '''deterioration of the tombs due to changes in groundwater and vegetation'''. As a result, the '''preservation zone was expanded by 3,000 square meters''', incorporating '''Kofun No. 56'''. To ensure the proper '''preservation and integration of the tumulus into the historic park''', the '''Kashiwara City Board of Education conducted an excavation in 1990 (Heisei 2)''', leading to a '''full investigation''' of the site. === Kofun === The '''northern side of the tumulus''' has been eroded due to '''urban development''', creating a '''steep cliff''', while the '''western side was partially removed''' to construct a '''road'''. The '''southern and eastern portions''' remain largely intact, but much of the original '''mound soil has been lost'''. While '''some evidence suggests it may have been a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)''', it is currently classified as a '''22-meter diameter round mound'''. Numerous '''cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa''' (totaling nine pieces) were recovered. These are believed to have '''fallen into the chamber due to collapse from tomb robbers'''. === Yokoana-style Stone Chamber === [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Takaidayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Developmental Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] Excavation revealed a '''large number of stones''', confirming the existence of a '''Yokoana-style stone chamber'''. The chamber was constructed within a '''shallowly-dug elliptical burial pit (8m east-west, 9m north-south)'''. The '''chamber structure''' features '''flat slab stones stacked in a "katasedo" (片袖式) design''', which is '''one of the earliest types of Yokoana-style burial chambers in Kinki'''. Notably, the tomb shares similarities with '''{{ill|Fujinomori Kofun|ja|藤の森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujinomori Kofun|qq=}}''' and '''early Baekje tombs in Korea''', suggesting '''continental influences'''. Archaeologists such as '''Shiraishi Taichirō''' classify this chamber as '''one of the earliest Yamato-style Yokoana chambers''', while others, like '''Ichinose Kazuo''', argue that its '''broader chamber width represents an early stage in the development of large Kinki-style stone chambers'''. === burial goods === Although the site was '''heavily looted''', the excavation revealed '''two wooden coffins''', identified by '''iron brackets''' and the distribution of grave goods. Notable burial goods include: * '''Gold earrings (one from the central coffin, two from the eastern coffin)''' * '''Chinese-made "Shinjin Ryūko Kyō" (神人龍虎image鏡) bronze mirror''' (diameter: 20.6cm) * '''Uttō (熨斗, iron-shaped bronze implement)''' * '''Iron swords and daggers''' * '''Glass beads''' (some featuring '''gold foil between inner and outer layers''', a rare type found in Japan) * '''Armor:''' Helmet with a '''projecting horn (衝角付冑)''', neck guard (頸甲), shoulder guards (肩甲), '''lamellar cuirass''' * '''Weapons:''' Spears, halberds, iron arrowheads * '''Horse gear:''' Stirrup fragments, bits * '''Tools:''' Sickle, axe, chisel * '''Ceramics:''' Numerous '''Sue pottery''' and '''Haji pottery''' pieces Some burial goods, such as the '''Uttō''', are rarely found in Japan (only four cases) but are common in '''5th-century Baekje tombs''', including '''King Muryeong's Tomb''' (武寧王陵). The '''gold-foil glass beads''' are similarly rare in Japan, with only '''five kofun examples''' but are '''commonly found in Muryeong’s tomb'''. In '''2008 (Heisei 20), all excavated burial goods were collectively Historic Site1 as a Kashiwara City Tangible designation1'''. == designation1 == === Kashiwara City Designated designation1 === * '''Tangible designation1''' ** '''excavations from Takaidayama Kofun''' - Stored at '''Kashiwara City Historical Museum''', Historic Site1 on '''March 21, 2008'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|Takaida Cave Tombs|WD=Q11668714|lt=Takaida Cave Tombs}} * {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|ja|平尾山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takaidayama Kofun}} * [http://www.skao.net/yokoken/data/kawachi.html Kinki Region Yokoana Stone Chamber Data Collection - Osaka Kawachi Area] (Yokoana Stone Chamber Research Group) {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} (shōkaku kabuto) 高井田山古墳出土 横矧板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} from {{ill|Takaidazan Kofun|ja|高井田山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaidazan Kofun|qq=}}
